demands.rationale: REWE and Edeka expanded BIO and 'Aus Berlin' shelves; demand for hyper-local hydroponic lettuce strong.

sources.snippet: REWE Berlin's regional sourcing program expanded to 14 hydroponic-lettuce SKUs in 2025.

demands.rationale: Mediterranean-restaurant density in Mitte and Kreuzberg; supermarket fresh-herb sections expanding.

sources.snippet: Berlin gained 320 net Italian-restaurant openings between 2020 and 2025.

demands.rationale: Berlin's vegan-fine-dining scene (FREA, Lucky Leek) drives premium microgreens demand. Local producers limited.

sources.snippet: Berlin's vegan fine-dining sector grew faster than any other European capital between 2022-2025.

demands.rationale: German consumers willing to pay 30%+ premium for off-season local strawberries vs Spanish imports.

sources.snippet: Off-season strawberry imports to Germany hit €280M in 2024, with retailers eager to substitute.

demands.rationale: High per-capita consumption in Germany; baby-spinach hydroponic varieties command retail premium.

sources.snippet: German spinach consumption averages 0.36 kg/person/month, second highest in EU.

demands.rationale: Significant Vietnamese, Chinese, Korean immigrant population (Mitte, Wedding); growing fine-dining demand.

sources.snippet: Berlin's Asian-cuisine restaurant count grew 28% in three years.

demands.rationale: German-cuisine staple (Quark, Frankfurter Grüne Soße); supermarket fresh-herb constant demand.

sources.snippet: Chives remain Germany's #2 fresh herb by volume, behind parsley.

demands.rationale: Pizza/salad menu staple in Berlin's Italian sector; Edeka reports double-digit growth on rucola SKUs.

sources.snippet: Edeka rucola sales grew 14% YoY in 2024; supply mostly imported from Italy.
